title: Capnorchis formosa
taxonomy: Capnorchis formosa is a type of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The plant is classified as a species within the genus Capnorchis. The scientific classification is further divided into kingdom Plantae, followed by division Magnoliophyta, class Liliopsida, order Asparagales, and family Orchidaceae. This classification system helps in understanding the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

intro: Capnorchis formosa, also known as the Chinese club moss, is a type of gymnosperm plant that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae. This plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, which include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exerting antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Capnorchis formosa include lignans, alkaloids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Capnorchis formosa is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
